formations_blocks: Turkey possession in a structured build-up/attack-vs-defence phase
Australia mid block early, then dropping into a deep defensive third / low block with 11 men behind the ball
Turkey high press when Australia tried to play out from the back
adjustments: Australia briefly jump from their mid block to press in midfield
Turkey slow, conservative circulation through midfield and the right side
Australia settling into compact box defending around set pieces
key_matchups: Calhanoglu vs Australia’s defensive block from range
Guler vs Australia’s edge-of-box pressure, including Toure robbing him
Metcalfe vs Turkey’s defensive recovery on right-sided delivery
phases.summary: The opening quarter-hour is dominated by Turkey’s possession and territorial control, with Australia largely forced into a disciplined low block and attacking only sporadically. Australia’s best moments come from brief presses, corners, and isolated counter-press wins, while Turkey probe patiently through Calhanoglu, Guler and the right side without yet creating a clear breakthrough.

formations_blocks: Australia playing out from the back
Australia compact defensive web / compact structure
adjustments: Australia tried to unlock Turkey via the right channel after beating the press
Turkey continued dominating possession but needed more dynamism and a rethink of Plan A
key_matchups: Toure vs Turkey’s right-side defenders in the right channel
Bos vs his man on the left
Calhanoglu controlling play against Australia’s compact midfield/defensive block
Kadioglu attacking from outside the box against Australia’s defensive shape
phases.summary: Turkey controlled possession and pressed aggressively, but Australia’s compact defensive web largely blocked passing lanes and forced them into slower, methodical attacks. Australia showed bravery in playing out under pressure and threatened in transition, then capitalised on a right-sided opening to score through Irankunda in the 27th minute.

formations_blocks: Turkey set up in a possession-dominant but strikerless structure
Australia more dangerous on the counter than Turkey in possession
adjustments: Turkey may need to introduce Uzun or Gül to add an attacking presence in the box
Australia could consider bringing on Mabil or Volpato for Metcalfe
Australia are advised to keep pumping it up to Irankunda and Toure on the break
key_matchups: Irankunda and Toure in transition against Turkey's defence
Australia's defensive discipline against Turkey's possession and crossing
phases.summary: Australia reach half-time 1-0 up, with Tony Popovic’s major calls paying off and the Socceroos on course for a knockout-clinching result. Turkey have looked tidier and more technical, but their strikerless setup has left them short of bodies in the box and struggling to turn possession into clear chances, while Australia look more threatening on the counter and remain well placed if they keep their defensive shape.

formations_blocks: Turkey pressing higher after the break
adjustments: Turkey make a half-time substitution: Yilmaz off, Kenan Yildiz on
Turkey get Yildiz involved down the left more directly
Turkey increase their press and try to sustain pressure around the edge of the box
Australia look to exploit transitional moments and Irankunda's pace
key_matchups: Irankunda vs Turkey's retreating defence in transition
Yildiz vs Australia on Turkey's left side
phases.summary: Turkey came out of the break with a clear tactical tweak, introducing Kenan Yildiz and pushing him into the left-sided attacking role to increase their threat between the lines and in possession around the box. Australia, meanwhile, remained dangerous in transition, repeatedly using Irankunda's pace and left-sided combinations to break past a Turkey side that was pressing higher but also being tested by direct counters and set pieces. Much of the phase was shaped by Turkey's corner pressure and Australia's ability to survive it, with Souttar repeatedly dominant in the air at both ends.

formations_blocks: Australia defending in a compact low block / deep defensive block
Turkey camped in Australia’s half, recycling possession in front of Australia’s defence
Turkey attempting vertical attacks and long balls over the top
adjustments: Turkey introduced Ozcan and Muldur at 80'
Australia introduced Irvine and Behich at 83'
Australia’s defensive approach after the second goal shifted further into game-management and clearance mode
phases.summary: Australia’s 75th-minute strike from Metcalfe doubled the lead and allowed them to settle into a deep, disciplined defensive shape. Turkey responded by flooding Australia’s half, but the Socceroos repeatedly blocked central progression, defended crosses and vertical passes with bodies in the way, and forced increasingly frustrated efforts from range. Late substitutions on both sides did little to change the pattern: Turkey lacked imagination, while Australia protected their advantage through compact team defending and clearances.
